move.

mean, if you looked at it already, yeah, basically we're trying to just like, like they, you know, some of that stuff, like we've helped them write some of the stuff they just write internally.

think aligns with like how you want to market.

mostly it's like, this seems interesting.

more of the common kind of accepted kind of state of things.

Did
Do you guys do that kind of stuff?

Like, nobody really fully knows.

like, track all the prompts you care about and the journeys you care about.
And then you see what's being cited and you see what's being mentioned.
And then we just try to, like, get into those places over time.

They do a search and they, from that result, they kind of pull the things that I think is relevant.
So the majority of it looks like search, just, like, tailored a little bit more for, like, that specific prompt.
And, yeah, and that can be things like just, like, how the content is structured, like you said.
Or, like, we've seen some things where it's just.
It's like almost very trivial.

It has this word and it has in 2025.
So it's like, okay, so let's just do this little optimization, you know?
So we do that.
A lot of it looks like SEO.
A lot of the things we're experimenting with is more on the side of like, I don't know, like LLM.2xt, does that actually do anything?
It could look like structuring and FAQs and table of contents.

There's things that look like backlinking a little bit or at least the traditional kind of sense of...

Link building, but now it's more about like, we care about this prompt, know, like here are the five articles that it cites, can we just kind of get in those, however way we can, you know, pay the author, just email the company, trade them a link in one of our articles.
Like we spend most of our time on the like, free purchase kind of realm.
But I think we've also seen just like, like, after somebody is aware of your brand, you know, they search all this, all this stuff, like, does it integrate with this?
Does it do A or B?
How does it compare with this product?
And there's like, this whole other realm that kind of like is owned by product marketing that we're also trying to do more of as well.
Like for, actually, this is our client, Webflow.
We do this, like, if you go to webflow.com slash integrations, like all those guides are done by us.
And a lot of that's attempting to like, somebody asks the questions like, hey, can Webflow integrate with Stripe?
Or like, can I build this with Webflow?
Like, that stuff is just meant to feed that.
So, yeah, I know if I answered your question.
It did.
We actually, we were not sophisticated whatsoever in those early days.
At Quadrant, when we started, we were like on the fourth page of Google or something like that.
And we were not even mentioned in the output of GPChat, GPT, or anything like that.
And we literally brute forced our way into the results.
We just had like an army of writers that we would outsource to for content to Medium blogs or Dev2 blogs and all these other locations.
And we just made sure to keep a consistent story.
And same story with LensDB.
When we started, you would ask whether LensDB was like a production-ready database.
And it said, no, you just only use this for prototyping, which, you know, we were going crazy over that.
Because we needed to make sure that when somebody's building an app, it's an embeddable database.
So, it needs to be embedded.

By Claude, as you're building the app, and we made sure that all the necessary content where you teach people, you can embed this in your program, you can do it this way, this way, and we literally just flooded as much content as we could.
We were, it's still the Wild West.
I mean, to say that we knew exactly what to do would be to lie.
We just needed to.

Yeah, mean, like, same with everyone else, you know, like, there's a lot of, like, the prompt tracking companies tend to market saying they know exactly how it works, but, like, I think the truth is, like, nobody knows, you know.

I mean, that's kind of interesting with the LanceDB stuff, but one of my friends is the founder of Olama, and I think they're also, like, they started as, like, run your model locally for, like, little experiments or whatever, but if they want to get into more production, it's, like, there's so much content on the internet that says otherwise, and you're trying to just basically, like, change that, you know, however you can, like, make Reddit posts, change your website, you know, post on Dev2 and Medium, like you said.
One of the things that's being done right now, Elastic, is posting big time on Reddit because they have an entire team of people, you know, just surveying all their subreddits.
It seems to be the new hot thing.
Yeah, it seems to be working.
I mean, we track a ton of prompts.
It seems like the Reddit stuff is more, like, trained in.
Like, we see less and less of Reddit threads actually getting cited now than before, but clearly it's, like, shaping the answer somehow.
It's just, like, not totally visible.
One of the things, just to sum this up, you know, I started writing content, but I was not into marketing in my early days.
But now that I've been working in growth, I see the biggest difference between content and growth is being able to adapt yourself.
Always be on your toes and see what works, track your performance, and always employ the newest methods.
Not get stuck in your ways.
That's the worst thing.
I absolutely agree.
I mean, I think...
Because it's changing so fast.

So like as a company, we're trying to essentially like productize this thing we're doing, you know, like, I don't know if you look too much in the space with startups or building, but like this concept of like, you know, software is eating the world, but now software is eating kind of labor in a lot of places.
And like what marketers do is like they write words, right?
And I think, you know, like a lot of it can be broken down into some kind of AI assisted or purely agentic kind of workflow.
So, so that's what we're doing.
And I think the interesting part about it is like to the customer, they're buying a service.
So like none of these like patterns of work is like encoded in a product that you have to then update if it changes.
We're just kind of in the middle of figuring that out.
And then hopefully one day, you know, we release the product and actually a question on that.
Do you, so like you, you run a service now and then, like you, you have like a team that kind of does this kind of DevRel tier content for folks.
Is that the.
I'm not just a DevRel anymore.
We're building production-level applications and adjusting existing ones for people.
So throughout this DevRel process, I did become a developer throughout the process.
So I realized that you can't just write content.
You have to build a thing, and then you write about it.
So I ended up teaching myself how to build these agentic applications in search.
So I have some customers in e-commerce and some enterprises building their internal agents.
So that's probably about half the business, and the other half, I'm advising two companies currently on GTM.
I think you probably know what they are.
I don't know if I want to go public with that just yet.
But they're getting very popular these days, and they're using Quadrant and Lands, building agents also, some frameworks and tools out there.
So I'm advising those on GTM, and I'm putting content together for those guys as well.
So I have some of my old team members that I'm working with, people who are kind of senior level machine learning engineers.
They're helping me out.

I guess we have a lot of, I guess the reason I ask is like we have a lot of customers in this realm that, you know, for the most part, like I think our workflows and our processes are really good at creating kind of like SEO tier content.
But once you get into this realm of like have an opinion, build something, like can't really do that purely with LMs.
And we lack kind of this expert in the loop that can kind of guide our team on the content to write.
So for example, like search as a customer, it sounds like maybe we should try something there.
We have, I don't know if you know the company Base 10.
They're also a customer.
My buddy Kareem runs sales at Base 10.
We're doing content for them.
That engagement just kicked off and like, you know, they have like a really high bar and like what we're looking for.
they just got the money, right?
They're doubling the size.
did get a big amount of money.
Yeah.
So I guess if you do have capacity to do more, like there are other things to also work on.
So just let me know, I guess I can send you a list of all the other projects we have going on.
You let me know if it's something you could potentially.
I'd like to, if you're interested, of course, I'd like to start on something where I can do my best work.
Probably data, anything related to data, data retrieval, you know, agentic applications, even training, post-training stuff is good for me as well.
However you see this working out, I think, you know, let's try it if you're interested.
Just starting small probably works best.
Yeah, then let us, I think we'll, I'll invite you to a little shared Slack channel.
But we have like a few topics where we can kind of give you the vision, I guess, for what we're trying to do.
Like kind
Write a post on this topic, and then maybe we can go from there.
Is there like a model you typically use to work with folks, like hourly, retainer, buy article?
How do you typically work with folks?
I work with a retainer, but I can send you a few options that I have laid out for some other clients.
I'll share them with you, and then we can work out a deal.
But yeah, I'm interested.
And yeah, I just have to think about it a little bit.
I saw the logos on your site.
Maybe you can tell me which companies you'd have some capacity for, or you'd need.
Yeah, I think we'd probably just start with Surge.
Like you said, let's just do like a little concept trial on that.
And then it seems like more and more we're getting like some companies that are for developers in the AI, like stack somewhere.
So Face 10 is one.
And then the
I'll look at who else we have, but there's plenty.
Okay.
All right.
So we've decided that you're going to invite me to a Slack channel.
I'll send you kind of a pricing list and we can work out a deal, hopefully.
Okay.
That sounds good.
Yeah.
We'll just kick it off with like, just try to write something and see how it goes.
Sounds good.
Yeah.
Happy about that.
It works for me.
All right.
All right, guys.
Liz, Jason, thank you so much.
And hope to talk to you soon.
Yeah.
Great to meet you.
See you.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Discuss potential collaboration on technical writing projects for GrowthX's clients, particularly Surge AI.](https://fathom.video/share/Xr8qgzYuNZAVRcwRL-ax36ZhWjddbEfC?tab=summary&timestamp=297.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [David Myriel, an experienced technical writer and DevRel expert, is interested in collaborating with GrowthX on AI-related content creation](https://fathom.video/share/Xr8qgzYuNZAVRcwRL-ax36ZhWjddbEfC?tab=summary&timestamp=206.0)
  - [GrowthX is exploring ways to create more in-depth, technical content for clients like Surge AI, focusing on topics such as post-training, benchmarking, and data labeling](https://fathom.video/share/Xr8qgzYuNZAVRcwRL-ax36ZhWjddbEfC?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0)
  - [The collaboration will start with a small trial project for Surge AI, with potential for expansion to other GrowthX clients in the AI/developer space](https://fathom.video/share/Xr8qgzYuNZAVRcwRL-ax36ZhWjddbEfC?tab=summary&timestamp=1352.0)

## Topics

### David Myriel's Background and Expertise

  - [10 years of technical writing experience, originally from economics/finance background](https://fathom.video/share/Xr8qgzYuNZAVRcwRL-ax36ZhWjddbEfC?tab=summary&timestamp=206.0)
  - [Worked at Quadrant (vector databases), growing from initial team to head of DevRel with 7-person team](https://fathom.video/share/Xr8qgzYuNZAVRcwRL-ax36ZhWjddbEfC?tab=summary&timestamp=229.0)
  - [Led growth at LanceDB, focusing on VectorSearch](https://fathom.video/share/Xr8qgzYuNZAVRcwRL-ax36ZhWjddbEfC?tab=summary&timestamp=257.0)
  - [Currently running a consultancy, working with clients in e-commerce and enterprise on building agentic applications and GTM strategy](https://fathom.video/share/Xr8qgzYuNZAVRcwRL-ax36ZhWjddbEfC?tab=summary&timestamp=1131.0)

### GrowthX's Content Creation Approach

  - [Focus on SEO and content for LLMs, expanding into DevRel territory](https://fathom.video/share/Xr8qgzYuNZAVRcwRL-ax36ZhWjddbEfC?tab=summary&timestamp=122.0)
  - [Repurpose existing content (e.g., podcast appearances) into more comprehensive blog posts](https://fathom.video/share/Xr8qgzYuNZAVRcwRL-ax36ZhWjddbEfC?tab=summary&timestamp=497.0)
  - [Track prompts and optimize content for AI model citations and suggestions](https://fathom.video/share/Xr8qgzYuNZAVRcwRL-ax36ZhWjddbEfC?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
  - [Employ various tactics: SEO optimization, Reddit engagement, link building, and product marketing content](https://fathom.video/share/Xr8qgzYuNZAVRcwRL-ax36ZhWjddbEfC?tab=summary&timestamp=798.0)

### Potential Collaboration Areas

  - [Creating blog posts (500-1000+ words) based on existing ideas or podcast content](https://fathom.video/share/Xr8qgzYuNZAVRcwRL-ax36ZhWjddbEfC?tab=summary&timestamp=638.0)
  - [Focusing on topics like post-training, benchmarking, data labeling, and AI model performance](https://fathom.video/share/Xr8qgzYuNZAVRcwRL-ax36ZhWjddbEfC?tab=summary&timestamp=410.0)
  - [Potentially expanding to other GrowthX clients in the AI/developer space (e.g., Base 10)](https://fathom.video/share/Xr8qgzYuNZAVRcwRL-ax36ZhWjddbEfC?tab=summary&timestamp=1236.0)

### Content Optimization for AI Models

  - [Discussion on strategies to improve content visibility in AI model outputs](https://fathom.video/share/Xr8qgzYuNZAVRcwRL-ax36ZhWjddbEfC?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
  - [Importance of adapting to new methods and tracking performance](https://fathom.video/share/Xr8qgzYuNZAVRcwRL-ax36ZhWjddbEfC?tab=summary&timestamp=1052.0)
  - [GrowthX's efforts to productize their content creation process with AI assistance](https://fathom.video/share/Xr8qgzYuNZAVRcwRL-ax36ZhWjddbEfC?tab=summary&timestamp=1081.0)

### Next Steps for Collaboration

  - [Jason to invite David to a shared Slack channel](https://fathom.video/share/Xr8qgzYuNZAVRcwRL-ax36ZhWjddbEfC?tab=summary&timestamp=1307.0)
  - [David to send pricing options for retainer-based work](https://fathom.video/share/Xr8qgzYuNZAVRcwRL-ax36ZhWjddbEfC?tab=summary&timestamp=1324.0)
  - [Start with a small trial project for Surge AI](https://fathom.video/share/Xr8qgzYuNZAVRcwRL-ax36ZhWjddbEfC?tab=summary&timestamp=1352.0)
  - [Explore potential work for other GrowthX clients in the AI/developer space](https://fathom.video/share/Xr8qgzYuNZAVRcwRL-ax36ZhWjddbEfC?tab=summary&timestamp=1236.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Jason to invite David to a shared Slack channel for communication](https://fathom.video/share/Xr8qgzYuNZAVRcwRL-ax36ZhWjddbEfC?tab=summary&timestamp=1307.0)
  - [David to send pricing options for retainer-based work to Jason](https://fathom.video/share/Xr8qgzYuNZAVRcwRL-ax36ZhWjddbEfC?tab=summary&timestamp=1324.0)
  - [GrowthX to provide specific topics and vision for initial Surge AI content project](https://fathom.video/share/Xr8qgzYuNZAVRcwRL-ax36ZhWjddbEfC?tab=summary&timestamp=1307.0)
  - [Begin trial collaboration on a small-scale project for Surge AI](https://fathom.video/share/Xr8qgzYuNZAVRcwRL-ax36ZhWjddbEfC?tab=summary&timestamp=1352.0)
  - [Evaluate results and discuss potential expansion to other GrowthX clients](https://fathom.video/share/Xr8qgzYuNZAVRcwRL-ax36ZhWjddbEfC?tab=summary&timestamp=1352.0)

action_items.description: Invite David to shared Slack; share Surge topics + Edwin podcast links; request 500–1000-word draft
